children.heading: Projects by qualified youth or conservation corps
children.label: 1 Projects by qualified youth or conservation corps
children.text: The Secretary is authorized to pay not more than 75 percent of the costs of any appropriate conservation project carried out pursuant to this subchapter on public lands by a qualified youth or conservation corps. The remaining 25 percent of the costs of such a project may be provided from nonfederal sources in the form of funds, services, facilities, materials, equipment, or any combination of the foregoing. No cost sharing shall be required in the case of any appropriate conservation project carried out on Indian lands or Hawaiian home lands under this subchapter.

children.heading: Public Lands Corps projects
children.label: 2 Public Lands Corps projects
children.text: The Secretary is authorized to accept donations of funds, services, facilities, materials, or equipment for the purposes of operating the Public Lands Corps and carrying out appropriate conservation projects by the Corps. However, nothing in this subchapter shall be construed to require any cost sharing for any project carried out directly by the Corps.

children.heading: Funds available under National and Community Service Act
children.label: b Funds available under National and Community Service Act
children.text: In order to carry out the Public Lands Corps or to support resource assistants and qualified youth or conservation corps under this subchapter, the Secretary shall be eligible to apply for and receive assistance under section 121(b) of the National and Community Service Act of 1990 [].42 U.S.C. 12571(b)

children.heading: Other funds
children.label: c Other funds
children.text: Amounts appropriated pursuant to the authorization of appropriations under are in addition to amounts allocated to the Public Lands Corps through other Federal programs or projects.section 1730 of this title
